title: buxus microphylla var. platyphylla
intro: Buxus microphylla var. platyphylla, also known as Japanese boxwood or boxwood, is a variety of the Buxus microphylla plant, a small shrub or tree native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of alkaloids such as buxine. The health benefits of Buxus microphylla var. platyphylla include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Buxus microphylla var. platyphylla include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments and promote overall health and well-being.
